title: Build your Presentation
description: Arrange fragments to build each phase of a presentation.

sentences.prompt: Build the opening about renewable energy
sentences.correct: Good morning. I'm going to talk about renewable energy.

sentences.prompt: Build a transition to the second point
sentences.correct: Moving on to my second point, let's look at the data.

sentences.prompt: Build the closing of your presentation
sentences.correct: In summary, the key takeaway is that renewable energy matters.

sentences.prompt: Describe a chart to the audience
sentences.correct: As you can see in this chart, sales increased by 20%.
Does anyone have any questions about what I've presented?

sentences.prompt: Thank the audience at the end
sentences.correct: Thank you for your attention. I'm happy to take questions.

title: What Comes Next?
description: Choose the best continuation for each moment of the presentation.

questions.question: You've just finished your introduction. What do you say to start the first point?
options: First of all, let's look at...
questions.explanation: "First of all, let's look at..." is the standard transition to your first main point.

questions.question: You've just finished your second point. What do you say?
questions.explanation: "Moving on to..." is a smooth transition between points.

questions.question: "As you can ___ in this graph, sales increased by 20%."
questions.explanation: "As you can see" is the standard phrase for describing visuals.

questions.question: Someone asks a question you don't know the answer to. You say:
options: "I'll look into that and get back to you."
questions.explanation: "I'll look into that and get back to you" is professional and honest.

questions.question: What is the BEST way to start a formal presentation?
options: "Good morning. My name is... Today I'm going to talk about..."
questions.explanation: A formal opening includes greeting + name + topic clearly stated.

questions.question: "___ on to our next topic, let's discuss the budget." (Moving/Jumping)
questions.explanation: "Moving on to..." is the standard transition phrase between topics.

questions.question: You want to emphasize a key finding. You say:
questions.explanation: "I'd like to highlight that..." draws attention to important information.

questions.question: What is a "key takeaway" in a presentation?
questions.explanation: The key takeaway is the main message you want the audience to remember.

questions.question: "To ___, the three key points are: cost, quality, and speed." (summarize/begin)
questions.explanation: "To summarize" signals you are wrapping up the main points at the end.

questions.question: Which phrase is best for giving an example during a presentation?
questions.explanation: "For instance" introduces a concrete, credible example.

title: Presentation Phrases
description: Master the essential phrases for each phase of a presentation.

cards.front: GOOD MORNING. I'M GOING TO TALK ABOUT... 🎬 (Opening)
cards.back: Standard formal opening. Greeting + topic. Level: Formal.
cards.example: Good morning. I'm going to talk about climate change.

cards.back: I would like to start with... Flexible and professional opening.
cards.example: I'd like to start by sharing some interesting data.

cards.back: Moving on to... Transition between main points. Level: Semi-formal.
cards.example: Moving on to our second topic, let's discuss the budget.

cards.back: Now let's move on to... Alternative to "moving on to". Level: Formal.
cards.example: Now let's turn to the results of our survey.

cards.front: AS YOU CAN SEE IN THIS CHART... 📊 (Visual)
cards.back: As you can see in this graph... To describe visual support.
cards.example: As you can see in this chart, sales grew by 15%.

cards.back: I would like to highlight... To emphasize a key point.
cards.example: I'd like to highlight that customer satisfaction increased.

cards.back: The key point is... Summarize the most important idea.
cards.example: The key takeaway is that sustainability drives growth.

cards.back: To summarize... The formal closing of the presentation begins.
cards.example: To summarize, we need to invest in three key areas.

cards.back: Excellent question! Save time and validate the questioner.
cards.example: That's a great question. Let me think about that for a moment.

cards.front: I'LL LOOK INTO THAT AND GET BACK TO YOU. ❓ (Q&A)
cards.back: I will look into it and get back to you. For when you don't know the answer.
cards.example: I'll look into that and get back to you after the meeting.

cards.back: For example... Introduce a concrete example during the presentation.
cards.example: For instance, our Tokyo office increased sales by 30%.

cards.back: Thank you for your time. Courteous and professional closing.
cards.example: Thank you for your attention. Any questions?

title: Phrase → Presentation Phase
description: Match each phrase with the phase to which it belongs.

pairs.left: Good morning, today I'll talk about...
As you can see in this chart...
